title: The Silence After the Argument
content: The argument itself lasted less than fifteen minutes. The silence that followed lasted for weeks. Neither Emma nor Noah could remember exactly how the disagreement had begun. They only remembered the words spoken too quickly and the pride that kept either of them from reaching out first. Days became strangely empty. Every song, every familiar café, and every evening walk reminded them of conversations that were no longer happening. Eventually, Noah realized that winning an argument had never been as important as protecting the relationship they had spent years building. When he finally knocked on Emma's door, neither of them started by explaining who had been right. Instead, they admitted how much they had missed each other's presence. They discovered that forgiveness rarely arrives because someone proves a point. It begins when two people choose understanding over pride.
